<question>

Ta

Ta ; R

<question>
1. a is ivory.
2. a is south.
3. If a is west and a is ivory, then b is ruby.
4. If b is ruby, then b is north.
5. If a is south and a is ivory, then b is elm.
6. If b is elm, then b is west.

15. If b is east and b is poppy, then c is maple.

19. If c is west and c is laurel, then d is granite.
20. If d is granite, then d is west.
21. If c is east and c is orchid, then d is pearl.
22. If d is pearl, then d is west.
23. If c is south and c is cedar, then d is harbor.
24. If d is harbor, then d is north.
25. If c is west and c is maple, then d is willow.
26. If d is willow, then d is west.
27. If d is west and d is granite, then e is lime.
28. If e is lime, then e is west.
29. If d is west and d is pearl, then e is olive.
30. If e is olive, then e is north.
31. If d is north and d is harbor, then e is birch.
32. If e is birch, then e is east.
33. If d is west and d is willow, then e is elm.
34. If e is elm, then e is east.
Which state applies to e?
</question>

Qa

Qc & Bc -> Fd
Fd -> Pd
Sc & Ic -> Od
Od -> Sd
Sd & Ed -> Je
Je -> Se
Pd & Fd -> Ae
Ae -> Re
Sd & Od -> De
De -> Se
</premises>
<proof>
Ga ; R
Qa ; R
Db ; ->E
Sb ; ->E
Hc ; ->E
Sc ; ->E
Ed ; ->E
Sd ; ->E
Je ; ->E
Se ; ->E
</proof>
<conclusion>
Se
</conclusion>
</formal>
<answer>
olive
</answer>
